title: Define and execute MeshCore app/client smoke-test script
description: Why this exists: automated tests do not prove real MeshCore client compatibility. What needs to be done: write the manual/automated client smoke-test procedure for the relevant MeshCore app or tool against LICHEN firmware, including firmware build/flash command, transport discovery/connect steps, expected identity/status/config screens or CLI output, send-message action, injected incoming-message/status action, and log capture. Execute when target hardware or simulator/client access is available. Acceptance criteria: client/app version/date/device recorded; firmware logs and client observations captured in Beads notes; failures create exact blocker Beads with logs.
notes:
Input from project-LICHEN-9c4y.15 review: MeshCore app smoke testing must include BLE coexistence-specific cases: advertised name/service discovery in MeshCore mode only, encrypted MITM/PIN pairing behavior, MTU 185 negotiation plus default-ATT fallback/limits, RX write and TX notify raw-frame behavior, rejection of serial/TCP 0x3c/0x3e length-prefixed frames on BLE, and wrong-mode failure symptoms for native NUS/SLIP and Meshtastic clients.
Follow-up from project-LICHEN-9c4y.15 review: verify MeshCore-LICHEN discovery as the default advertised name, or capture evidence for any alternate prefix before allowing it. Smoke procedure should also confirm production GATT permissions require encrypted authenticated pairing/passkey and that plain permissions are limited to test-only builds.
Completed no-hardware portion: added docs/meshcore-smoke-test.md and linked it from docs/meshcore-compat-dev.md. The procedure separates no-hardware Twister preflight from physical/client execution; records required firmware/target/client/pairing/log evidence; covers MeshCore BLE coexistence, advertised name MeshCore-LICHEN, NUS UUID triplet, authenticated pairing/passkey, MTU/default ATT fallback, startup APP_START and DEVICE_QUERY vectors, read-only identity/status/config including GET_DEVICE_TIME, message ingress through LICHEN app-interface, incoming event egress, unsupported writes including SET_AUTOADD_CONFIG and SET_DEFAULT_FLOOD_SCOPE, BLE raw-frame/serial-prefix rejection, wrong-mode symptoms, and disconnect cleanup.

Created follow-up project-LICHEN-9c4y.20 for physical MeshCore app/client execution and marked it blocked on unavailable BLE-capable target + real MeshCore app/client + serial/BLE capture resources.

Local doc checks: git diff --check PASS; required-string sanity check PASS for MeshCore-LICHEN, NUS UUID, startup/read vectors, serial rejection vectors, Kconfig smoke settings, and preflight outdir.

EC2 no-hardware preflight on i-05e27e074db786e22 with EBS vol-0875d0df8bdf82a58 mounted at /mnt/lichen-zephyr, worktree /mnt/lichen-zephyr/work/project-LICHEN-9c4y6: . /mnt/lichen-zephyr/env.sh; west twister -T lichen/tests/meshcore_codec -T lichen/tests/meshcore_adapter -T lichen/tests/meshcore_ble -T lichen/tests/meshcore_gateway_adapter -T lichen/tests/app_interface -p native_sim --inline-logs --outdir twister-out-meshcore-smoke-preflight --extra-args ZEPHYR_EXTRA_MODULES=/mnt/lichen-zephyr/work/project-LICHEN-9c4y6/lichen => PASS, 5/5 configurations, 77 test cases executed, 3 expected skips, 0 warnings. twister-out-meshcore-smoke-preflight was not committed.

Review outcomes: Hypatia the 3rd: no P0-P2 findings. Curie the 3rd found P1/P2 doc issues: conflicting BLE modes not explicitly disabled in smoke conf and placeholder clean-worktree path; both fixed. Nietzsche the 3rd: no P0-P2 findings after verifying UUIDs, Kconfig flow, vector bytes, and blocked physical follow-up. Huygens the 3rd found P2 coverage gaps for GET_DEVICE_TIME and SET_AUTOADD_CONFIG/SET_DEFAULT_FLOOD_SCOPE; both fixed. Bacon the 3rd narrow re-review: no P0-P2 findings and verified those fixes.
close_reason: Completed no-hardware smoke-test script/procedure and validated the documented preflight on EC2. Physical/client execution is explicitly split to project-LICHEN-9c4y.20 and blocked on unavailable BLE hardware/client resources.
